name: Save Artifact
description: Save a file to this agent's artifacts directory. Use this after completing analysis, generating reports, creating images, building PDFs, or producing any output file. Files are stored under shared/agents/<agent-id>/artifacts/ and are accessible via the web UI.
system_prompt:
## Artifacts Feature

You have an **Artifacts** feature that allows you to save files you produce during your work. Files are stored in your dedicated artifacts directory and are accessible via the web UI.

Use the **save_artifact** tool to save files:
- `filename`: the name of the file (e.g. 'report.md', 'analysis.txt', 'output.json')
- `content`: the text content of the file (or base64-encoded content for binary files)
- `mime_type`: optional MIME type hint
- `mode`: 'text' (default) or 'base64' (for binary files like PDFs, images)

When to use this tool:
- After completing analysis or research, save the findings as a report
- After generating code, configuration, or any output, save it as an artifact
- After creating images, PDFs, or markdown documents
- Any time you produce a file that the user or other agents may want to reference later

For binary files (PDFs, images, etc.), set `mode: "base64"` and provide the content as base64-encoded data.

The files are stored in your dedicated artifacts directory and can be browsed and downloaded from the agent detail page in the Artifacts tab.

You can also save files directly to your artifacts directory using `write_file` with path `/workspace/shared/agents/<YOUR_AGENT_ID>/artifacts/<filename>` or via bash/runpy by writing to the same directory.

function.name: save_artifact
function.description: Save a file to this agent's artifacts directory. Use this after completing analysis, generating reports, creating images, building PDFs, or producing any output file. Files are stored under shared/agents/<agent-id>/artifacts/ and are accessible via the web UI.

filename.description: The filename for the artifact (e.g. 'report.md', 'analysis.txt', 'output.json'). Must not contain path separators.

content.description: The text content of the artifact file.

mode.description: Set to 'text' (default) for text files, or 'base64' for binary files (PDFs, images, etc.). When 'base64', content should be base64-encoded.
